name: extract-transcripts
description: Extract readable transcripts from Claude Code and Codex CLI session JSONL files
capabilities: Extract Claude Code session JSONL files to readable markdown
Extract Codex CLI session rollout files to readable format
Index and search across multiple sessions using DuckDB
Filter sessions by date, project, or keyword
Include or exclude tool calls and thinking blocks from output
Batch process all sessions in a directory
author: 0xBigBoss
securitySummary: Pure Python file processing utility that reads local session JSONL files and outputs formatted markdown. No network access, no code execution, no credential exposure. All static findings are false positives from pattern matching on documentation examples and legitimate file paths.
valueStatement: Claude Code and Codex CLI store conversation history as JSONL files that are difficult to read manually. This skill extracts those sessions into clean markdown format with metadata, timestamps, and optional tool call details.
userTitle: Convert session logs to readable transcripts
limitations: Requires Python 3 with optional DuckDB dependency for indexing
Does not modify or delete original session files
Only reads from local session paths (no remote fetch)
Does not extract token usage or cost data

useCases.title: Session Review
useCases.description: Review past AI assistance sessions to recall decisions, context, or code patterns

useCases.title: Generate Guides
useCases.description: Convert session logs into tutorial content or process documentation

useCases.title: Audit Usage
useCases.description: Search across team sessions to identify patterns or verify implementation approaches

promptTemplates.title: Extract a session
promptTemplates.scenario: Convert Claude Code session to markdown
promptTemplates.prompt: Extract the Claude Code session at ~/.claude/projects/myproject/abc123.jsonl to a readable transcript

promptTemplates.title: Search sessions
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me all Claude Code sessions from the last week that mention authentication

promptTemplates.title: Batch export
promptTemplates.scenario: Convert all sessions in a project
promptTemplates.prompt: Extract all Claude Code sessions from ~/projects/api-server into markdown files

bestPractices: Use --summary flag for quick session overviews when you only need metadata
Use --skip-empty to filter out warmup-only test sessions during batch processing
Run transcript index to enable full-text search across your session history
antiPatterns: Do not rely on session IDs as unique identifiers - subagents can share IDs
Do not expect token counts or cost data - this information is not stored in session files
Do not modify original JSONL files - the extractor only reads from them

faq.question: What platforms are supported?
faq.answer: Claude Code CLI, Codex CLI, and Claude Code desktop app on macOS, Linux, and Windows.

faq.question: How large can sessions be?
faq.answer: Sessions are limited by your disk storage. Individual transcript output truncates messages over 2000 characters.

faq.question: Can I integrate with other tools?
faq.answer: Yes, scripts output to stdout or files. Pipe output to other tools or redirect to files for integration.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: All processing is local. No data is sent anywhere. The index stores transcripts in a local DuckDB database.

faq.question: Why are sessions not appearing?
faq.answer: Check that session paths exist. Use --path to specify custom directories. Run index --full to force re-scan.

faq.question: How is this different from native exports?
faq.answer: Native exports provide raw JSONL. This tool formats conversations as readable markdown with metadata and context.
